taziri_nkhaleb.home: The road itself—feels wrong when stationary more than two days
taziri_nkhaleb.appearance: Imazuran woman, late 40s, olive skin weathered by road dust, sharp brown eyes that count everything, ochre and grey traveling robes, silver measuring-chain belt, ink-stained hands
taziri_nkhaleb.and_yet: Keeps Isarna shrine in lead wagon, burns expensive incense at every crossroads
taziri_nkhaleb.wants: Profitable predictable routes, respect from both cultures, vengeance on lizard-men
taziri_nkhaleb.does_not_want: Questions about husband's death, choosing factional sides, lizard-men mentioned as allies
taziri_nkhaleb.oddly_also: Collects obscure road songs in journal, refuses water drawn south of the road
taziri_nkhaleb.family: Late husband Irven (Elunad, killed by lizard-men 8yr ago), son Khalid (24, leads return caravans), daughter Sarit (22, manages Orziben warehouses), son Davin (19, Ilmenor scribe apprentice), brother Azghar (teamster)
taziri_nkhaleb.self_image: Bridge between Gallaecian and Imazuran, keeper of husband's legacy, protector of her caravan family
taziri_nkhaleb.recreation: Singing road songs at crossroads, updating ledgers by candlelight, polishing husband's waystone marker
taziri_nkhaleb.dreams: Davin becomes respected Ilmenor scholar, safe profitable routes forever, finding who killed Irven
anecdotes: Once outbid a hobgoblin warlord for iron nails using only dried figs and honey
Navigated through fey-glamoured woods by singing Isarna hymns until dawn
Refused Covenant-Bound 'special cargo' offer despite massive payment
tragedies: Husband Irven killed in southern swamps 8 years ago (believes lizard-men responsible)
Has hidden map of 'safe paths' Irven drew before death—guards it obsessively
Receives coded letters from Davin hinting at dangerous Ilmenor secrets
taziri_nkhaleb.pc_leverage: Prove lizard-men didn't kill Irven OR find actual killers; protect Davin; share intelligence on humanoid coordination; respect her authority on the road

caravan.route: Caelrun to Tagmira (primary), broader Luric Marches circuit
caravan.current_leg: Caelrun to Tagmira — party traveling with her
caravan.brother_azghar: Teamster, Taziri's brother, manages the wagons day-to-day
caravan.waystone_observance: Will stop at Waystone of Isarna west of Tagmira for offering (bread, silver thread)
caravan.dm_notes: Taziri is the party's most reliable civilian connection across the Luric Marches. She knows both Caelrun and Tagmira well and can brief the party on surface-level politics of either. She has no knowledge of the undead alliance. Her Isarna observance at the waystone is a natural scene transition into Tagmira.

pima.and_yet: Possesses a mirror that allows her to communicate with a fairy girl on the other side
pima.wants: The Bronze Host defeated; protection for Tagmira
pima.pc_leverage: She has asked the party to defeat the Bronze Host on behalf of Tagmira

fairie_connection.mirror: A magic mirror Pima found, enabling two-way communication with fairie
fairie_connection.fairy_girl: A fairy girl on the other side of the mirror; identity unknown beyond being Pima's contact
fairie_connection.aislith: The fairy queen, described to Pima by the fairy girl
fairie_connection.dm_notes: The nature and origin of the mirror is unknown. The fairy girl's relationship to Aislith and motivations for reaching out through Pima are unestablished.

mother_yaleth.home: Nemetra—has never left, refuses to, sees leaving as betrayal of the sick and dying who need her
mother_yaleth.appearance: Late 60s, weathered face with deep lines, sharp grey eyes that miss nothing, faded green shawl, earth-stained hands, walks with carved hazelwood staff, wears dried herb bundles at belt
mother_yaleth.wants: Truth about northern woods threat, village to take danger seriously, protect the younger herb-wives
mother_yaleth.does_not_want: Militia interference, fools dismissing her warnings, goblins harmed before she learns what they know
mother_yaleth.oddly_also: Heavy smoker (pipe, strong tobacco) despite it damaging her lungs—considered unfeminine, she doesn't care
mother_yaleth.family: House Yebrin (distant cousin), 6 herb-wives under her guidance, no living children
mother_yaleth.self_image: Proud of her humility, quietly smug about being right when others are wrong
mother_yaleth.recreation: Smoking her pipe on the compound steps at dusk, watching village life
mother_yaleth.dreams: Establish Nemetra as tobacco exporter, prove herb-wives' wisdom to the fools, die respected
anecdotes: Delivered Captain Durvin himself during a winter storm—he was blue when he came out, she breathed life back into him. He's never thanked her once.
Diagnosed House Halbet's pottery master with lung rot from kiln smoke 20 years ago—he ignored her, kept working, died within the year. His son listens now.
Once talked down a delirious wounded orc at the forest edge using only hand signs and offering him water. Militia wanted to kill it. She sent it limping north with bandaged leg.
tragedies: Lost all three of her children (two daughters, one son) to fever in a single week during Year 285. She knows every herb that might have saved them—none grew close enough in time.
Her husband left her 40 years ago, said she 'loved plants more than people.' He remarried in Tagmira. She never speaks of him but keeps his tobacco pouch.

Her mentor, Old Kessa, was killed by militiamen who thought she was consorting with fey (she was gathering moonlit herbs). Yaleth found the body. Durvin's father led that patrol—she's never forgotten.
mother_yaleth.pc_leverage: Bring her proof of northern woods threat, protect goblins from militia, share intelligence she can't gather herself, respect her knowledge

leeandra.home: A hut in the swamp, several hours southwest of Nebruz
leeandra.appearance: Human female; full description not yet established at the table
leeandra.and_yet: Brews potions and pursues ring-lore with the steady patience of a craftsman, not the urgency of a power-seeker
leeandra.wants: Peace between the lizard-man tribes; freedom to pursue ring-lore; outsiders who respect swamp politics
leeandra.does_not_want: Tribal war in the Luric marches; outsiders treating the swamp as enemy territory
leeandra.oddly_also: Maintains a close friendship with Dorota of Nebruz — an unusual trust neither explains
leeandra.pc_leverage: Deliver the upland herbs from Mother Yaleth (done, session 27); retrieve giant constrictor snake brain fluid for ring-lore; demonstrate respect for lizard-man tribal politics and the swamp's balance

current_business.brew: Water-breathing potions for the party (4-day brew)
current_business.ingredient_needed: Giant constrictor snake brain fluid — party tasked with retrieval (session 27)
current_business.dm_notes: Load-bearing for two arcs: the water-breathing brew (4-day clock) and the constrictor hunt (session 28 driver). Diplomatic keystone of the Luric marches — her neutrality prevents lizard-man tribal war and keeps tribes from raiding Caelrun.

phaid_torngrasp.home: Nebruz — the Marsh-Hand's residence, which doubles as village hall
phaid_torngrasp.appearance: Human male, late 30s. Missing the pinky and index fingers on his right hand ('Torngrasp'). The reason is disputed.
phaid_torngrasp.wants: Nebruz to remain unnoticed, free, and self-governing
phaid_torngrasp.does_not_want: Outside authorities; anything that draws attention to Nebruz
phaid_torngrasp.pc_leverage: Earn community standing; bring problems through proper Nebruz channels; avoid making him look weak before the village

verfred_oot.appearance: Human male; former press-gang boss, built like one
verfred_oot.wants: A quiet house and paying customers
verfred_oot.pc_leverage: Pay your tab; don't start trouble; he has knock-out drops and is not shy about using them

borgul_ramshead.appearance: Human male; towering, heavyset, former slaver
borgul_ramshead.pc_leverage: He does not rent to adventurers; bribery or a strong reputation in Nebruz might change this

dandal_the_shaver.appearance: Human male; sharp eyes, enjoys a negotiation more than the sale
dandal_the_shaver.pc_leverage: Come prepared to haggle; know your goods; never show desperation

adlaba_kurn.appearance: Human female, early 40s; warm and popular with locals and dryboots alike
adlaba_kurn.wants: A full house and a good night's take
adlaba_kurn.pc_leverage: Drink her bog water; tip well; she knows everything that happens in Nebruz

gerum_o_the_spike.appearance: Human male; left hand replaced with a metal hook
gerum_o_the_spike.wants: Order kept; alligators fed on schedule
gerum_o_the_spike.does_not_want: Dryboots causing scenes near the pit
gerum_o_the_spike.pc_leverage: Respect the post; don't make his job harder; he enforces Phaid's judgments, not his own opinions

schall_the_honest.appearance: Human male; balding, plagued by psoriasis, bulging eyes
schall_the_honest.wants: Fresh catches; exotic specimens for the right buyer
schall_the_honest.pc_leverage: Bring him exotic catches — giant crayfish, swamp eels, giant crabs — and he pays well and asks no questions

sorga.pc_leverage: Bring her unusual swamp kills — she pays well for exotic hides

etienne.appearance: Human female; hide-stained hands, serviceable leatherwork
etienne.pc_leverage: High-quality exotic skins command her respect and best prices

wilem_stonenose.wants: To remain unfound; to keep his secondary trade quiet
wilem_stonenose.does_not_want: Anyone using Thieves' Cant; guild contact of any kind
wilem_stonenose.pc_leverage: Ask in plain language; prove you're not guild-connected; his under-the-counter stock is an open secret in Nebruz

dorota.home: A cottage on the outskirts of Nebruz, closer to the swamp than the boardwalks
dorota.appearance: Seemingly human female, late twenties in appearance; fae blood means she is considerably older than she looks
dorota.and_yet: Most of her charms and love potions are effective placebos. Her healing is entirely real.
dorota.wants: Village health; the balance between Nebruz and the swamp tribes maintained; her friendship with Leeandra undisturbed
dorota.does_not_want: Conflict between Nebruz and the swamp tribes; the temple reopened
dorota.pc_leverage: Respect the swamp; she is the informal channel to Leeandra for those Leeandra has not yet agreed to meet

effemi_nuha.home: Arms & Iron smithy, Nebruz — the only stone building in the village
effemi_nuha.appearance: Short human woman; corded arm muscles, close-cropped hair. Iron ring on right hand (enchanted against divination). Communicates in grunts and monosyllables.
effemi_nuha.and_yet: MU9 — a former imperial wizard who forged enchanted blades for nobility. Presents entirely as a simple smith. Dorota knows; Effemi does not know Dorota knows.
effemi_nuha.wants: Anonymity; her past buried; to keep smithing in peace
effemi_nuha.does_not_want: Anyone from her former life finding her; any reason to reveal her magical abilities
tragedies: A courtly coup stripped her of her imperial position and set magical and political rivals on her trail
effemi_nuha.pc_leverage: Ordinary circumstances will not move her; an overwhelming threat to Nebruz might force her hand; her smithwork is excellent despite low-grade bog iron

sermoner_peryl.home: A small boat on the river; visits Nebruz every two weeks
sermoner_peryl.appearance: Human male; the burning intensity of someone who believes he is winning a losing argument
sermoner_peryl.wants: To reopen the Nebruz temple; converts; to be proven right
sermoner_peryl.does_not_want: The Marsh-Hand's decree to stand forever
sermoner_peryl.family: A small congregation of Nebruz converts (names unestablished)
sermoner_peryl.pc_leverage: His congregation is a quiet information network; he helps those who might influence the Marsh-Hand or who show genuine interest in his faith

kalo_xyln.home: A poorly maintained house in Nebruz; will not leave after dark
kalo_xyln.appearance: Human male; missing both legs from the knee down, moves on short crutches. Eyes that expect the worst.
kalo_xyln.wants: Scraps; to survive the night; for the footsteps outside his door to stop
kalo_xyln.does_not_want: To be outside after dark; to speak of what was done to him
tragedies: Captured by a renegade band of lizard-folk (exiles from the treaty tribes) who tortured him and ate his legs over several days. Leeandra drove them off and returned him to Nebruz. His body survived; his mind did not.
Believes his severed legs return each night to walk outside his door and collect the rest of him.
kalo_xyln.pc_leverage: The renegade lizard-folk band is still at large in the swamp — distinct from the treaty tribes. Kalo is the only known survivor who has seen them. Getting him to speak requires extraordinary trust or extreme circumstances.

ossek_thrice_buried.home: Itinerant; uses a back room in a Caelrun fishmonger's he does accounts for
ossek_thrice_buried.appearance: Small, back-injured and crooked, ink-stained scar-ridged fingers. Moves with the careful deliberation of bad knees. Always: an overfull satchel, paper rolls in his belt, lamp-oil smell. Pale eyes that are always reading something, even when looking at you.
ossek_thrice_buried.and_yet: Can walk past Ilmenor inquisitors, tomb guardians, and militia captains on nothing but confident tone and improvised credentials. People believe him immediately and regret it later.
ossek_thrice_buried.wants: To read Ameniseb's tomb inscriptions — believes his work on conditional immortality connects to deliberately suppressed pre-Silence arcane knowledge.
ossek_thrice_buried.does_not_want: To fight, climb, run, or share credit.
ossek_thrice_buried.oddly_also: Collects small ceramic animal figurines from ruins — not for value, just because he likes them. Dozens wrapped in cloth in his satchel.
ossek_thrice_buried.family: Two adult sons, Jevek and Danek, in Velgroth-Lucernia — they think he is dead, or wish he were. A niece in Caelrun who leaves food out for him.
ossek_thrice_buried.self_image: The most competent man in any given ruin. Physically diminished, intellectually unmatched. Has earned every scar.
ossek_thrice_buried.recreation: Annotating his own notes. Arguing with texts. Writing marginalia he will never send.
ossek_thrice_buried.dreams: To find something that rewrites accepted history of the Silence — not for glory, just to know.
anecdotes: Convinced an Ilmenor inquisitor his grimoire was a recipe book by reading from it in a made-up dialect for four unbroken minutes.
Emerged from the second collapse after three days underground — dehydrated, one broken rib — still clutching his rubbing. 'The rubbing was worth it.' No one agrees.
Identified a sealed vault in Caelrun's lake ruins from waterlogged timber patterns during a drought — without entering the water. Sold the information before learning what was inside. Still annoyed.
tragedies: Nevret — young Aurka, one season in, killed in the first collapse. Ossek got out. Keeps Nevret's iron stylus in his breast pocket; doesn't discuss it, except constantly, in passing, as though Nevret might walk in.
Bressul — steady, experienced, survived everything, killed in the second collapse. Ossek invokes him most when pointing out load-bearing features: 'Bressul always checked those first.'

Davanek — killed two years ago in the third collapse. Ossek still refers to him in the present tense occasionally, catching himself with a small cough. He has not noticed the pattern, or does not consider it relevant.
ossek_thrice_buried.pc_leverage: Needs Zardun's hands, eyes, and feet — traps, locks, moving quietly when something turns out to be home. Offers: tomb layout knowledge, inscription reading, Aurka contacts, and the judgment to abort before it goes wrong. Will not hesitate to run; is not ashamed of this. Will mention Nevret, Bressul, and Davanek with unsettling frequency in contexts that make Zardun wonder whether they also thought the partnership sounded like a good idea.

ossivan_carn.home: Velgroth — travels a circuit through the Marches, available for hire at feast days and disputes
ossivan_carn.appearance: Well-dressed for the road. Clean hands. Writes everything down in a small bound ledger he keeps inside his coat.
ossivan_carn.and_yet: Genuinely believes he is helping. Is not wrong often enough to be dismissed.
ossivan_carn.wants: The record to be accurate. His reputation to precede him. Repeat business.
ossivan_carn.does_not_want: People who won't accept a fair verdict.
ossivan_carn.oddly_also: Excellent memory for names, debts, and slights — his own and others'.
ossivan_carn.family: Wife in Velgroth who manages his affairs there. Two daughters he mentions with precise, performative pride.
ossivan_carn.self_image: The most impartial man in any room.
ossivan_carn.recreation: Writing up cases. Corresponding with other lay-judges. Winning arguments he replays in his head.
ossivan_carn.dreams: A permanent seat in Velgroth's civic court. Recognition that the Marches need proper law.
anecdotes: Once arbitrated a land dispute between two families who had been feuding for thirty years. Both left furious. Both complied. He considers this a success.
Was run out of a village near Lucernia after a verdict went badly wrong — he maintains the verdict was correct, the village was wrong to ignore it.
tragedies: The verdict near Lucernia. He doesn't discuss details. He has discussed the principle at length.
ossivan_carn.pc_leverage: He can be hired, cited, or invoked — Lugus's law is neutral, and Ossivan follows it. He can also be pointed at enemies. The problem is he cannot be controlled once engaged, and he keeps notes on everyone.

tazuln.home: A stone shelter north of Orziben, built into a hillside. Eleven small holes in the ceiling, deliberate.
tazuln.appearance: Age unclear. Milky eyes that move constantly. Clumsy. Hands covered in small scars.
tazuln.and_yet: Never loses anything. Always knows who is in the room before they speak.
tazuln.wants: A student who won't flinch at what the other worlds look like.
tazuln.oddly_also: Always working on a stew, and breaks bread with unseen beings.
tazuln.recreation: Sitting very still, watching the worlds.
tazuln.dreams: To find a portal at one of the twenty vertices before he dies.
anecdotes: Described the interior of a sealed Ilmenor vault in Imazuran in precise detail. He has never been inside it. The Ilmenor have not commented.
Walked off a roof once. Got up, dusted off, said 'that ledge is much lower in the fourth world.' Has not elaborated.
tragedies: All his friends live in other worlds so he only gets to see them through a glass, darkly.
tazuln.pc_leverage: Will teach Chekkad that illusions are not fabrications but resonance — light and form bleeding through from adjacent worlds.
